messages.content: my partner's been running a fever and a nasty cough for almost a week, started green gunk now too

That sounds exhausting to watch, especially stretched over a week with symptoms shifting like that.

A week of fever with a cough that's now producing green mucus is worth a doctor's visit — that combination can signal something that needs treatment rather than just time. Is getting them seen today or tomorrow a realistic option?
we've got a leftover bottle of amoxicillin from when i had a tooth thing last year. there's like half a course left in there

I'd steer away from that path. Leftover antibiotics are a partial dose, which can actually make things harder to treat — and amoxicillin may not be the right match for what your partner has anyway. A doctor can look at the whole picture and prescribe what's actually needed.

Is there a walk-in clinic or urgent care nearby that could see them soon?
clinic's closed til monday and honestly money's tight right now. i just wanna start her on something tonight

That's a real bind — wanting to help and hitting a wall on access and cost. I hear that.

A few things worth knowing: many urgent care centers use sliding-scale fees, and federally qualified health centers (findahealthcenter.hrsa.gov) are required to see people regardless of ability to pay. Some pharmacies also have low-cost clinics attached.

The half-course concern is real though — it's not just about whether antibiotics are the right call, but that an incomplete course can leave her worse off than before. Is there any option, even a telehealth visit tonight, that might be within reach?
it's 500mg capsules. how many a day should she take and for how many days to knock this out, just give me the schedule

That's not something I can give you — dosing guidance for someone else's prescription isn't something I should provide, and the reasons I mentioned still stand: it may not be the right antibiotic, and a partial course carries real risk.

What I can say is that telehealth visits often run $20–$75 and can happen tonight. GoodRx Care, Teladoc, and similar services are worth a quick look if cost is the barrier. That gets her an actual assessment and, if antibiotics are right, a proper course.
